title: Fix(graph): Allow any Line subclass as edge_type in Graph/DiGraph
body:
Overview:
- Extend support so that Graph and DiGraph can accept any subclass of Line as edge_type, not just Line. This enables using subclasses like LabeledLine as edge_type without breaking edge construction.

Rationale:
- Previously, edges were instantiated as edge_type(self[u].get_center(), self[v].get_center(), z_index=-1, **edge_config). This relies on positional arguments, which breaks for Line subclasses that expect named parameters (e.g., LabeledLine which can take label=start/end via kwargs).
- The fix uses keyword arguments start=..., end=... (and passes through edge_config), allowing edge_type to be any Line subclass that accepts start/end as keyword parameters. For LabeledLine, providing a label via edge_config remains possible.

Changes:
- Update edge instantiation paths to use keyword arguments so any Line subclass can be used as edge_type.
- Specifically modify edge creation in Graph._populate_edge_dict, Graph._add_edge, and DiGraph._populate_edge_dict to construct edges with start=... and end=... keywords (instead of positional arguments).

Testing:
- Tests updated to cover LabeledLine as edge_type in test_graph.py, ensuring edge creation works when edge_type is a subclass of Line and when per-edge labeling is provided via edge_config.

Impact:
- Backwards-compatible for typical Line usage (Line as edge_type continues to work).
- Enables broader customization with edge_type subclasses, including labeled or otherwise augmented edges.

step_plan.summary: Extend Graph and DiGraph to accept any Line subclass as edge_type by constructing edges with start and end as keyword arguments, enabling LabeledLine and other subclasses to be used as edge_type without breaking edge creation.

steps.operation: Modify Graph._populate_edge_dict to instantiate edges using keyword arguments (start=..., end=...)
steps.reason: To support any Line subclass as edge_type by passing endpoints as keyword arguments instead of positional, enabling subclasses like LabeledLine to be constructed with edge_config parameters via keywords.
steps.side_effects: Requires updating tests to cover new keyword-based edge creation; preserves backward compatibility for standard Line usages; downstream code relying on positional edge construction may need review; ensures edge_type subclasses can receive extra kwargs (like label) via edge_config.

steps.operation: Modify Graph._add_edge to construct edge via keyword arguments start=..., end=..., and pass edge_config
steps.reason: Ensures all edge creation sites pass proper keyword arguments to edge_type across standard graph operations and remains compatible with edge_config.
steps.side_effects: Potential impacts on edge_type constructor expectations; may require adjustments in tests that rely on positional argument packing; ensures edge_config is correctly forwarded to edge_type.

steps.operation: Modify DiGraph._populate_edge_dict to instantiate edges using keyword arguments (start=..., end=...)
steps.reason: Extend DiGraph edge creation to support any Line subclass as edge_type by using keyword arguments, aligning with Graph changes and preserving behavior for directed graphs.
steps.side_effects: Tests and downstream code using DiGraph edge_type with kwargs should pass; ensure edge_config forwarding remains correct; minor performance/compatibility considerations are negligible.
